new_files.engine/mvcc/gc.go: EpochManager type (full struct starts line ~30), API: NewEpochManager, CurrentEpoch, AdvanceEpoch, AcquireCurrentEpoch, Release, RefCount, MinReferencedEpoch
new_files.engine/mvcc/gc_test.go: TestEpochRefcount (sequential bookkeeping, non-LIFO release order, double-release detection, MinReferencedEpoch transitions) + TestEpochRefcountConcurrent (goroutine stress under -race)

epoch_model_for_2a.2.2_2a.2.3.counter_semantics: uint64 starting at 1 (0 reserved as sentinel); epoch N = 'state of the world after the Nth AdvanceEpoch call'
intended_future_wiring: VersionWriter.CommitVersion (write.go) should call EpochManager.AdvanceEpoch() exactly once per successful CAS (not per retry attempt) so epoch boundaries align with a fileID's CurrentVersion actually advancing store-wide
Snapshot (read.go) NewSnapshot should call EpochManager.AcquireCurrentEpoch() and store the returned epoch on the Snapshot struct

A new Snapshot.Close() method (does not exist yet) should call EpochManager.Release(storedEpoch) — this requires updating 2a.1.3's TestSnapshotRead and 2a.1.5's TestConcurrentReadersWriters call sites to call Close(), OR deciding Close() is optional/best-effort; NOT decided in this subtask, left open for 2a.2.2
GC/compactor logic: a version superseded strictly before EpochManager.MinReferencedEpoch() (or when MinReferencedEpoch's ok==false, meaning no live snapshots at all) is safe to reclaim
epoch_model_for_2a.2.2_2a.2.3.explicit_tradeoff: As of this commit, EpochManager exists and is fully unit-tested in isolation but is NOT yet called from anywhere in write.go/read.go — refcounting is not yet load-bearing for real reads/writes. This was a deliberate scoping decision (see architecture-discovery.md/plan.md in this run dir) to avoid touching 2a.1.3/2a.1.5's existing passing tests within a subtask whose own acceptance criteria is only about the refcounting primitive itself.
